assertions.description: The Sanitizer interface is exposed on the global scope.
The Sanitizer constructor accepts an optional SanitizerConfig.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const s=new Sanitizer();const s2=new Sanitizer({elements:['p']});return s instanceof Sanitizer && s2 instanceof Sanitizer;}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: Element.prototype.setHTML is the safe sanitised HTML insertion entry point.

element.setHTML("

ok

") strips the
Element.prototype.setHTMLUnsafe is exposed for the unsanitised opt-in path.
Document.parseHTML is the static factory that returns a fully sanitised Document.
Sanitizer.prototype.get() returns the current configuration as an object.
assertions.test: (()=>{const s=new Sanitizer({elements:['p']});const g=s.get();return typeof g==='object' && g!==null;})()
